title: Flaky Spanish Duck Arroz Negro
description: A garlicky and flaky Spanish dish featuring duck legs, perfect for a sick day, made in a Dutch oven with a plant-based friendly twist, in the form of arroz negro.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced onion and cook for 3 minutes, until translucent.
Add the duck legs and cook for 5 minutes, until browned on all sides.
Add the Arborio rice and cook for 1 minute, until lightly toasted.
Add the vegetable broth, smoked paprika, saffron threads, and salt, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25 minutes.
Remove the duck legs from the pot and shred the meat into bite-sized pieces.
Return the shredded duck to the pot and stir to combine with the rice and broth.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
